glazes.name: Obsidian
glazes.description:
This 100% mixable celadon glaze is a deep, opaque black that pools and accents textured ware like the ancient glazes it is created to imitate.
Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.
*Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.
 
 10# DIPPING WARNING: This product can expose you to chemicals including Silica, crystalline (airborne particles of respirable size), which are known to the State of California to cause cancer. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov.

glazes.name: Supernova
glazes.description:
Supernova is a highly fluid creamy white translucent glaze with a crystalline surface like frost on a window. The gloss glaze surface is opalescent and breaks slightly more cream than white.
Apply to: Cone 04 BisquewareNumber of coats: 4 coats
This glaze performs best at cone 5 but can be fired to cone 6. For best results, leave the inside or back of the form unglazed.
Can be fired in a normal medium-speed firing with no special cooling schedule required. To take this glaze to the next level, use a controlled cooling schedule for increased crystal growth
These glazes are prone to running so we recommend stopping your glaze about half an inch above the foot. You can also use a drip tray or soft bricks to protect kiln shelves.

glazes.name: Black

images.alt: Image of CR-1 Black on a No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware tile fired to cone 5.

glazes.description:
CR-1 is a glossy black glaze that, when fired, crawls into raised shapes of varying sizes and forms. The result is a surface marked by unpredictable, natural patterns that add unique texture and visual interest to each piece. The featured chip image of CR-1 is applied on AMACO No.11 A-Mix White Stoneware.
Apply 1-4 even coats of glaze to cone 04 bisque. Allow each coat to dry almost fully, but before any significant cracking occurs. Crawl glazes will develop hairline cracks as they dry; handle with care. For added durability before firing, layer over an AMACO Celadon glaze or underglaze.
More coats create larger glaze dots, while thinner coats produce a finer bead effect. Increased coats will produce more cracks. Fire to cone 5-6 for best results.

The Kiln Ice glazes are modeled after traditional Guan or Snowflake glazes. They feature a delicate network of fine hairline cracks (known as “crazing”). Kiln Ice glazes are translucent, but become slightly opaque where the cracks form, creating a beautiful, multi-layered visual texture. Differences in glaze application and firing technique may affect end results.
These glazes are food safe, but due to durability, they are not recommended for food surfaces. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Emerald Falls
glazes.description: Emerald Falls is a beautiful glossy green glaze that breaks lighter and pools into a mauve grey-green. When heavier coats are applied this allows the glaze to float up and create interesting surface variations echoing mossy patches found in rolling emerald landscapes. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Art Deco Green
glazes.description:
Art Deco Green is a glossy, light blue-green glaze that floats metallic spots to the surface resembling a patinate copper roof.
Clay body and application can affect the size and abundance of the metallic spots.

glazes.name: Frosted Melon
Deep Sienna Speckle
Ancient Jasper
glazes.description:
Ancient Jasper is a fluid, opaque glaze that transitions from soft black to olive green to rust red as it is applied thicker. This glaze has an active, varied surface by itself and layers with our other Potter's Choice glazes with striking results.
Ancient Jasper benefits from a heavier application. This glaze will produce reds on all clay bodies, although iron-bearing clay bodies will produce the widest range of colors. No special cooling cycles or soaks are needed. Soaking can actually diminish the reds. 
Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.
*Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently

glazes.name: Chun Plum
glazes.description: Chun Plum is a pink-red translucent glaze that breaks light tan over textures. This glaze imitates the copper reds of Asia but with a reliable color response in oxidation firings. Layer over other Potters Choice glazes for a warmer tone and hints of pink and red. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Ancient Copper
Smokey Merlot
glazes.description: Smokey Merlot is a warm, dark purple glaze with a soft float of lilac over the surface. This glaze looks stunning on smooth surfaces, over textured surfaces, and layered with our other Potters Choice glazes. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Deep Firebrick
Textured Amber
glazes.description: Textured Amber is a semi-matte brown glaze that has a varied, translucent surface with a textured float of opaque tan speckles. Try layering this glaze with our other Potter's Choice glazes! Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Textured Amber Brown
glazes.description: Textured Amber Brown is a glossy brown glaze that breaks lighter where thinner like a traditional temmoku glaze. This glaze layers well with our other Potter's Choice glazes and produces fluid, beautiful effects.

glazes.name: Cosmic Tea Dust
glazes.description: Cosmic Tea Dust is a sparkly, dark brown glaze, which breaks a light yellow-brown over texture. The sparkle shifts subtly from silver to copper to give the glaze an iridescent quality. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.

glazes.name: Aventurine
glazes.description: Aventurine is an amber glaze with crystal flecks, that breaks golden over texture and pools a darker shade in recessed areas. This glaze is fluid so leave room for vertical flow. Testing is recommended to find the proper application and firing. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes. *Note that all dry dipping glazes can be difficult to layer. Dipping glazes behave differently from brushing and they require thorough testing prior to layering full piece.

glazes.name: Black Aventurine
Cosmic Oilspot
glazes.description: Cosmic Oil Spot is a dark glaze with shimmery flecks that create streams and spots of color similar to a traditional hare’s fur glaze. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.

glazes.name: River Rock
glazes.description: The sandy, dark brown tones of River Rock feather into a beautiful ebony before breaking into a rich amber when applied over texture. Subtle green-grey honeycomb shapes appear underneath the amber breaks when this glossy glaze is applied on vertical surfaces. Due to the powdered nature of the materials involved with the dry-mix dipping buckets of this product, their respective health information and labels differ from the brushing glazes.

glazes.name: Golden Honey
Cosmic Gold Dust

images.alt: PC-69 Cosmic Gold Dust Cone 6 Tile

glazes.name: Copper Red
glazes.description:
Copper Red is an oxblood red that gives the same results in oxidation as a traditional reduction copper glaze​. Copper Red breaks clear on edges or where thin.
Apply 3 coats for best results, do not thin glaze.
 
 WARNING: This product can expose you to chemicals including Lithium carbonate, which are known to the State of California to cause birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov.

glazes.name: Flambe
glazes.description:
Flambé​ is a copper red glaze with tones of purple and blue, that gives the same results in oxidation as a traditional reduction copper glaze. Flambé breaks clear on edges or where thin.
Apply 3 coats, do not thin glaze.
 
 WARNING: This product can expose you to chemicals including Lithium carbonate, which are known to the State of California to cause birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov.

glazes.name: Fire & Ice
Midnight Run
glazes.description:
PCF-3 Midnight Run is a glossy dark blue-gray glaze like a velvety night sky.
Breaks dark across textured surfaces. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Aurora Breeze
glazes.description:
PCF-16 Aurora Breeze is a hazy pink glaze with hints of blue and purple and dark speckles. It breaks across textured surfaces, developing a warm blush float when thick. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay.
Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.






Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO® Gum Solution if needed. Apply 3 even coats of glaze to Cone 04 bisque. (Let the glaze dry between coats.) Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. 



Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Honeydew
glazes.description:
PCF-18 Honeydew is a glossy pale green glaze like the melon it's named for. It breaks across textured surfaces and develops a peachy float when thick. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat.
Beautiful on any color of clay. Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Cirrus Flow
Ocean Drift
glazes.description:
PCF-21 Ocean Drift is a calming blue color, interspersed with fine, dark speckles that add texture and visual interest. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. 



Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6. 












Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO® Gum Solution if needed. Apply 3 even coats of glaze to Cone 04 bisque. (Let the glaze dry between coats.) Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. 



Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Mystic Tide
glazes.description:
PCF-33 Mystic Tide is earthy green, highlighted by a subtle, mottled texture that adds depth and complexity to the surface. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. 






Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6. 












Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO® Gum Solution if needed. Apply 3 even coats of glaze to Cone 04 bisque. (Let the glaze dry between coats.) Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. 



Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Rose Mist
glazes.description:
PCF-36 Rose Mist features a soft, blush-pink base, with subtle hints of lavender and white swirls. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay.









Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6. 












Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO® Gum Solution if needed. Apply 3 even coats of glaze to Cone 04 bisque. (Let the glaze dry between coats.) Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. 



Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Flux Blossom
Sunbeam
glazes.description:
PCF-60 Sunbeam is a radiant golden-yellow with subtle gradations of amber and honey. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. Beautiful on any color of clay. 



Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.















Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO® Gum Solution if needed. Apply 3 even coats of glaze to Cone 04 bisque. (Let the glaze dry between coats.) Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. 



Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Sun Shower
glazes.description:
PCF-63 Sun Shower is a pale yellow interspersed with fine, dark speckles. It breaks across textured surfaces and develops a warm blush float when thick. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat. 






Good at cone 5, more fluid at cone 6.















Shake well before use. Thin with water and AMACO® Gum Solution if needed. Apply 3 even coats of glaze to Cone 04 bisque. (Let the glaze dry between coats.)Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. 



Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: River Birch
glazes.description:
PCF-74 River Birch is a glossy cream glaze with runny dark specks like the bark of a tree. River Birch breaks across textured surfaces and develops a peachy float when thick. Great for layering with other glazes as a base or top coat.
Beautiful on any color of clay. Very fluid at cone 5, more so at cone 6.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Moss Mist
Night Rain
Teal Drift
Lunar Pink
Floating Lavender
Chai Gloss
glazes.description: Chai Gloss Shino is a glossy, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of cream, orange, and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Chai Matte
glazes.description: Chai Matte Shino is a matte, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of cream, orange, and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Acai Gloss
glazes.description: Acai Gloss Shino is a glossy, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of blue and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Acai Matte
glazes.description: Acai Matte Shino is a matte, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of blue and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Cacao Gloss
glazes.description: Cacao Gloss Shino is a glossy, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of brown and tan depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Cacao Matte
glazes.description: Cacao Matte Shino is a matte, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of brown and tan depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Oolong Gloss
glazes.description: Oolong Gloss Shino is a glossy, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of mint green and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Oolong Matte
glazes.description: Oolong Matte Shino is a matte, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of mint green and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Matcha Gloss
glazes.description: Matcha Gloss Shino is a glossy, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of green and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Matcha Matte
glazes.description: Matcha Matte Shino is a matte, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of green and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Hibiscus Gloss
glazes.description: Hibiscus Gloss Shino is a glossy, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of salmon pink, orange, and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Hibiscus Matte
glazes.description: Hibiscus Matte Shino is a matte, 100% mixable mid-fire glaze that yields wonderfully varied shades of salmon, orange, and brown depending on the thickness of application.

glazes.name: Black
glazes.description: Satin Matte Black is an opaque, buttery black glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance. Add SM-1 to other Satin Matte glazes to create shades of each color.

glazes.name: Stone
glazes.description:
SM-2 Stone is a dark taupe satin glaze, smooth as a weathered rock. 
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Marble
glazes.description:
SM-3 Marble is a pale gray satin glaze, just a whisper of color like the polished marble it's inspired by. 
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Clear Satin
glazes.description: Clear Satin is a smooth, buttery zinc-free clear glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance. Add SM-10 to other Satin Matte colors to create softer tones. Use 2 coats over underglazes for a more translucent result. Zinc-free.Some underglaze colors may be muted with SM-10 Clear Satin:  V-370 Velour Black V-361 Jet Black V-316 Light Pink

glazes.name: White
glazes.description: Satin Matte White is an opaque, buttery white glaze accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance. Add SM-11 to other Satin Matte glazes to created tinted colors.

glazes.name: Light Blue
glazes.description: Satin Matte Light Blue is an opaque, buttery robin's egg blue glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Dark Blue
glazes.description: Satin Matte Dark Blue is an opaque, buttery cobalt glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Seafoam
glazes.description:
SM-24 Seafoam embodies the serene beauty of the ocean, bringing a sense of calm and sophistication to any pottery creation.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Teal
glazes.description: Satin Matte Teal is an opaque, buttery blue-green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Blue Green
glazes.description: Satin Matte Blue Green is an opaque, buttery deep teal glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Mint
glazes.description: Satin Matte Mint is an opaque, buttery light green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Chartreuse
glazes.description: Satin Matte Chartreuse is an opaque, buttery pea-green glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Moss
glazes.description:
SM-46 Moss is a deep forest green, matte glaze with a hint of olive in the final color.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Red
glazes.description: Satin Matte Red is an opaque, buttery scarlet glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Cayenne
glazes.description:
SM-53 Cayenne is a spicy and warm red satin glaze with an earthy undertone.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Goldenrod
glazes.description:
SM-60 Goldenrod is a rich, deep yellow matte glaze like a summer flower.
Glaze fire to Cone 5-6. Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Yellow
glazes.description: Satin Matte Yellow is an opaque, buttery gold glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Orange
glazes.description: Satin Matte Orange is an opaque, buttery orange glaze that accentuates textured ware and boasts a classic art deco appearance.

glazes.name: Ivory Beige Underglaze
glazes.description:
Ivory Beige Velvet Underglaze is a soft cream color that is very light at cone 05. It warms up a little at cone 5, but burns out almost completely at cone 10. A clear glaze over Ivory Beige accentuates and darkens the color at cone 05 and cone 5. At cone 10, there is no color change between glazed and unglazed.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Terra Cotta Underglazes
glazes.description:
Terra Cotta Velvet Underglaze is a warm cinnamon brown that is much darker with a clear glaze on top at any temperature. At cone 05, it is red-brown and reminiscent of the clay it is named after. It darkens at cone 5 and becomes a deep chocolate color at cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Straw Underglaze
glazes.description:
Straw Velvet Underglaze is a rich sunflower yellow that is a strong ochre at cone 05 and becomes lighter at cone 5. At cone 10, it is even lighter and less saturated. Overall, it maintains its color well at any temperature and becomes richer and darker under a clear glaze.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Yellow Underglaze
glazes.description:
Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a bright lemon yellow that is vivid at cone 05. It is still bright, but less saturated at cone 05 and becomes pale at cone 10. A clear glaze over Yellow darkens the color slightly, but has less of an effect at higher temperatures.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Deep Yellow Underglaze
glazes.description:
Deep Yellow Velvet Underglaze is a rich ochre color that is very rich at cone 05 and is intense under a clear glaze. At cone 5, it is less saturated and becomes muted and darker at cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Nectar Underglaze
glazes.description:
Nectar underglaze is a warm yellow color like honeysuckle flowers at cone 05 and becomes more light amber under a clear glaze. It slightly intensifies at cone 5 and cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Red Brown Underglaze
glazes.description:
Red Brown Velvet Underglaze is a warm cocoa color that is reddish at cone 05. The color darkens at cone 5, and becomes chocolatey at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Chocolate Brown Underglaze
glazes.description:
Chocolate Brown Velvet Underglaze is a deep brown that is warmest at cone 05. The color darkens at cone 5, and is darkest but less saturated at cone 10. This underglaze looks great beneath a few coats of a clear glaze.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Peach Underglaze
glazes.description:
Peach Velvet Underglaze is a warm pink at cone 05 that darkens slightly at cone 5, and becomes a light coral at cone 10. A clear glaze darkens the color slightly at any temperature.
Peach Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under C-11 Mixing Clear.
Originally formulated to dilute the color of Celadon series glazes, C-11 Mixing Clear can be used as a clear glaze. Mixing Clear is a stiff gloss glaze that works well with most underglazes.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Coral Underglaze
glazes.description:
Coral is a warm, bright pink underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 10 with minimal darkening. Clear glaze brightens the color. Can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Rose Underglaze
glazes.description:
Rose Velvet underglaze is a deep magenta that is dark at cone 05, becomes patchy at cone 5 before it burns out almost completely at cone 10. A clear glaze over Rose will accentuate the color at cone 05 but at cone 5 and 10, there is no color change between glazed and unglazed.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Lavender Underglaze
glazes.description:
Lavender velvet underglaze is a periwinkle color that is soft at cone 05, becomes deeper at cone 5, and burns out at cone 10. A clear glaze over Lavender will accentuate the color at cone 05 and 5 and preserves some bluish tones from the underglaze at cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Lilac Underglaze
glazes.description:
Lilac velvet underglaze is a soft plum color that is light at cone 05 that becomes warmer and deeper at cone 5 and begins to burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze over Lilac will accentuate the color at cone 05 and 5 and will burn out a lot of the color at cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Purple Underglaze
glazes.description:
Purple Velvet underglaze is a violet color that is soft at cone 05, becomes bluish and lighter at cone 5 before it becomes very light and blue at cone 10. A clear glaze over Purple will accentuate the color at cone 05 and cone 5 and causes more blue tones at cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Salmon Underglaze
glazes.description:
Salmon Velvet underglaze is a soft peach color that is light at cone 05, becomes warmer and darker at cone 5, and maintains a toasted peach color at cone 10. A clear glaze over Salmon will accentuate and darken the color at cone 05, cone 5, and cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Blush Underglaze
glazes.description:
Blush is a warm, soft pink underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 10 with minimal darkening. Clear glaze brightens the color. Blush underglaze can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Baby Blue Underglaze
glazes.description:
Baby Blue Velvet underglaze is a soft light blue color that is bright and pastel at cone 05, becomes darker at cone 5, and is darker sky blue at cone 10. A clear glaze over Baby blue will accentuate the color at cone 05 but begins to burn out some of the color at cone 5 and cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Medium Blue Underglaze
glazes.description:
Medium Blue Velvet Underglaze is a brilliant primary blue that is vivid at cone 05, darker at cone 5, maintaining its blue color. A clear glaze over Medium Blue will significantly darken the color at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Turquoise Blue Underglaze
glazes.description:
Turquoise Blue Velvet underglaze is a blue green color that is cyan at cone 05, becomes deeper cone 5, and becomes very dark at cone 10. This underglaze maintains its color well under a clear glaze at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Iceberg Blue Underglaze
glazes.description:
Iceberg Blue Velvet underglaze is a light blue color that is powder blue at cone 05, becomes slightly darker at cone 5, and maintains its light blue shade up to cone 10. A clear glaze over Iceberg Blue tends to lift the color out slightly, lightening it.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Sea Glass Underglaze
glazes.description:
Sea Glass is a very pale blue-green color at cone 05 and becomes richer and darker under a clear glaze. It remains the same at cone 5, but fired to cone 10 becomes more blue.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Indigo Underglaze
glazes.description:
Indigo is a deep purple-blue  color at cone 05 and darkens under a clear glaze. When fired to cone 5, it becomes more blue in color and then darkens slightly at cone 10.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Teal Blue Underglaze
glazes.description:
Teal Blue underglaze is a deep blue color that is rich sapphire at cone 05 that becomes a deep medium blue at cone 5, and is very dark at cone 10. A clear glaze over Teal Blue will drastically darken its color at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Avocado Underglaze
glazes.description:
Avocado Velvet Underglaze is a pea green color that is vivid at cone 05, darkens at cone 5 and becomes a deep chartreuse at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
For Avocado Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift. 
HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Royal Blue Underglaze
glazes.description:
Royal Blue Velvet Underglaze is a navy blue that is deep and saturated at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes almost black at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Blue Green Underglaze
glazes.description:
Blue Green Velvet Underglaze is a teal color that is deep aqua at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes a dark marine blue at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Chartreuse Underglaze
glazes.description:
Chartreuse Velvet Underglaze is a lime green color that is very bright at cone 05, softens to a mint green at cone 5, and darkens slightly at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Light Green Underglaze
glazes.description:
Light Green Velvet Underglaze is a light spring green color that is bright at cone 05, darkens significantly to a forest green at cone 5, and becomes a deep pine green at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Pistachio Underglaze
glazes.description:
Pistachio is a light green underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 6 with minimal darkening. Above cone 6 the color becomes slightly bluer. Clear glaze brightens the color. Pistachio underglaze can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Cactus Underglaze
glazes.description:
Cactus is a cool light green underglaze that retains color from cone 05 up to cone 6 with minimal darkening. Above cone 6 the color becomes slightly bluer. Clear glaze brightens the color. Cactus underglaze can be used with zinc or non-zinc glazes without color shift.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Orange Underglaze
glazes.description:
Orange Velvet Underglaze is a golden color that is bright at cone 05, softens to a toasty beige at cone 5, and becomes toastier at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Dark Green Underglaze
glazes.description:
Dark Green Velvet Underglaze is a rich green color that is bright and saturated at cone 05, darkens to a forest green at cone 5, and becomes slightly darker at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
For Dark Green Velvet Underglaze, we recommend using HF-9 Zinc Free Clear. If HF-10 Clear is used, color may shift. 
HF-9 Zinc-Free Clear is recommended for use with underglazes to reduce the incidence of color shift. Most underglazes, stains, and coloring oxides will show their best color with Zinc-Free Clear.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Pearl Gray Underglaze
glazes.description:
Pearl Gray Velvet Underglaze is a bluish silver gray color that is bright at cone 05, darkens to a pewter tone at cone 5 and begins to lighten and burn out at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Ultra White Underglaze
glazes.description:
Ultra White Velvet Underglaze is a bright and opaque white at cone 05, a frosty color at cone 5, and maintains its vividness at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: White Underglaze
glazes.description:
White Velvet Underglaze is snow white color that is bright and minty at cone 05, softens at cone 5, and becomes slight warmer, but maintains a clean white color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the underglaze at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Jet Black Underglaze
glazes.description:
Jet Black Velvet Underglaze is a cool black color that is smooth and velvety at cone 05, darkens at cone 5, and becomes darkest black and slightly glossy at cone 10. A clear glaze deepens and accentuates the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Jet Black Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under SM-10 Clear Satin. 
SM-10 Clear Satin is a silky, stiff zinc-free clear glaze that works well as a liner or over underglazes. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Ultra Black Underglaze
glazes.description:
Ultra Black Velvet Underglaze fires to a beautiful soft, dark matte color at cone 05 then deepens to a darker black at cone 5 and maintains it’s shade at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Ultra Black Velvet Underglaze may be muted when used under SM-10 Clear Satin. 
SM-10 Clear Satin is a silky, stiff zinc-free clear glaze that works well as a liner or over underglazes. Use 2 coats over underglazes to show the color.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Morning Fog Underglaze
glazes.description:
Morning Fog Velvet Underglaze fires to a light, misty gray when fired to cone 05, darkens to a steamy color at cone 5, and maintains the foggy color at cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

glazes.name: Elephant Underglaze
glazes.description:
Elephant Velvet Underglaze is a light, ashy shade when fired to cone 05, darkens to a deeper gray at cone 5 then turns to a grayish-black akin to elephant skin when fired to cone 10. A clear glaze accentuates the color of the underglaze significantly at any temperature.
Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.

Due to variations in raw materials used during manufacturing, we recommend firing glazed test tiles before using each new batch number of glaze.
